etimesBengaluru cafe passes LPG hike to customers with ‘Gas Crisis Charge’ on lemonade; internet asks, “Did they boil it?”A Bengaluru cafe's bill for lemonade sparked online debate after adding a "Gas Crisis Charge." The Rs 17.01 fee, a 5% addition, appeared alongside the Rs 358 cost of two lemonades and GST, despite an Rs 18 discount. While some found the charge humorous, others questioned its legitimacy, though some defended it as a reflection of rising operational costs.

etimesIndian-German couple reveals why they left Germany and moved to India; internet surprisedAn Indian-German couple, finding life in Germany too predictable despite its comfort and security, has relocated to India seeking new challenges and personal growth. They emphasize their move wasn't due to weather or rules, but a desire for a more vibrant and transformative experience. Their story, shared online, has resonated with many.

etimesVideo: How much does it cost to live alone in Goa? Tech professional shares her Rs 82,000 monthly expensesA software engineer's monthly expenses in Goa have sparked discussion. Madhushree Joshi shared her Rs 82,000 budget, covering rent, car EMI, food, transport, and personal spending. Content creation helps offset some costs. Social media users debated the figure, noting variations in living expenses based on lifestyle and location. The breakdown offers insight into solo living in the popular Indian state.
